name: 3Commas
description_short: Leading crypto trading bot platform with DCA bots, grid bots, signal bots, and smart portfolio management across 20+ exchanges.
description_long:
## What 3Commas Is and Who Built It

3Commas is a crypto trading bot and automation platform that lets you build, backtest, and deploy algorithmic strategies across 20+ centralized exchanges without writing code. Founded in 2017 and headquartered in Tallinn, Estonia, the company operates as 3Commas Tech OÜ and has grown into one of the more recognizable names in the crypto bot space alongside Cryptohopper and Pionex.

The platform's core proposition is simple: connect your Binance, Bybit, OKX, Kraken, or Coinbase account via API keys, configure a bot from a library of templates (DCA, grid, signal, futures), and let it trade while you sleep. 3Commas has since layered in **QuantPilot**, an agentic AI system that builds, tests, and optimizes strategies end-to-end — a clear response to the wave of AI-first competitors entering the trading bot category in 2025-2026.

## Core Features That Actually Matter

The **DCA Bot** is 3Commas' flagship product and the most configurable tool on the platform. It supports multi-pair monitoring across 100+ trading pairs, letting you scan for setups that match your entry conditions rather than committing to a single asset. This is genuinely useful for traders who want to systematize dollar-cost averaging into dips rather than chasing manual buys.

The **Signal Bot** integrates with TradingView webhooks, which is the feature power users care about most. If you already have Pine Script strategies working on TradingView, you can pipe alerts directly into 3Commas and have them execute on your connected exchange. This bridges the gap between chart-based strategy design and live execution — something most standalone bot platforms handle poorly.

**Grid bots**, **futures bots**, and a traditional **SmartTrade** manual terminal round out the toolkit. Backtesting runs on full 1-minute historical data, which is more granular than many competitors who only offer hourly candles. The **AI Assistant** helps beginners configure bots by translating plain-English strategy descriptions into bot parameters, and it's better than most AI wrappers because it's constrained to actual 3Commas settings rather than hallucinating features that don't exist.

The portfolio tracker and performance analytics are competent but not standout — you're not buying 3Commas for its dashboards.

## Pricing: What You Actually Pay

3Commas uses a four-tier structure with meaningful gaps between plans:

**Free** ($0/month) gets you SmartTrade manual trading, **1 DCA bot**, the portfolio tracker, and the AI Assistant. It's genuinely useful for testing the platform but you'll hit the single-bot ceiling within a week if you're serious.

**Starter** ($29/month or $174/year — a **50% annual discount**) unlocks multiple bots, backtesting, and Signal bots. This is the tier most retail traders should start on.

**Advanced** ($49/month or $294/year) adds Grid bots, higher concurrent bot limits, and advanced analytics. Worth the jump if you're running grid strategies on sideways markets.

**Pro** ($99/month or $594/year) unlocks futures bots, unlimited bots, and priority support. At nearly $1,200/year, this only makes sense if you're actively trading leveraged perps and running dozens of concurrent strategies.

Annual billing saves you roughly 50% versus monthly — a steep enough discount that anyone planning to use 3Commas for more than three months should just commit annually.

## What 3Commas Genuinely Does Well

**Exchange coverage is best-in-class.** 20+ integrations including all the majors (Binance, Bybit, OKX, Kraken, Coinbase, KuCoin, Bitfinex) means you're rarely locked out because your exchange isn't supported. Competitors like Pionex tie you to their own exchange, which is a dealbreaker for anyone who already has capital elsewhere.

**TradingView Signal Bot integration is the killer feature** for anyone who lives on TradingView. Piping Pine Script alerts into automated execution genuinely bridges a workflow gap that other platforms fumble.

**Backtesting on 1-minute data** matters more than people realize. Bots that look great on hourly backtests often fall apart on minute-level execution because of slippage and false signals. Testing on the real granularity you'll trade at gives you a more honest performance estimate.

**The free tier is usable**, not a bait-and-switch. You can genuinely evaluate one DCA bot end-to-end before paying.

## Honest Limitations

**The 2022 security breach is the elephant in the room.** In October 2022, a phishing attack led to attackers accessing API keys of some 3Commas users and using them to execute unauthorized trades on connected exchanges. 3Commas has since hardened its systems, added IP whitelisting requirements, and rotated affected keys — but if you connect API keys, always use exchange-side withdrawal restrictions (never enable withdrawal permissions on any bot's API key, on any platform).

**Crypto only.** No stocks, forex, or futures on traditional markets. If you want a bot platform that spans asset classes, look elsewhere.

**The learning curve is real.** The interface has grown organically since 2017 and it shows — settings are nested across multiple screens, and configuring your first DCA bot without watching a tutorial is genuinely difficult. Newer platforms designed around AI-first UX (including 3Commas' own QuantPilot) are trying to fix this, but the core product still has legacy complexity.

**The Pro tier is expensive** at $99/month. If you're not running futures strategies or 10+ concurrent bots, you're overpaying versus Advanced.

**Custom indicators aren't supported** natively — you have to route through TradingView Signal Bot, which adds latency and requires a TradingView Pro subscription for webhook alerts.

**No journaling features.** If trade review and reflection are core to your process, you'll need to pair 3Commas with a dedicated journal like Edgewonk or TradeZella.

## Who Should Use 3Commas

**Good fits:** crypto-native traders who already have accounts on Binance, Bybit, or Kraken and want to automate DCA or grid strategies; TradingView power users who want to automate Pine Script signals; portfolio managers running multiple concurrent strategies who value exchange breadth.

**Bad fits:** complete beginners who haven't picked a strategy yet (the platform assumes you know what you want to automate); stock or forex traders (wrong asset class entirely); anyone unwilling to properly secure exchange API keys with IP whitelisting and no withdrawal permissions.

## 3Commas vs Cryptohopper vs Pionex

**Cryptohopper** is the closest direct competitor. It offers a strategy marketplace and copy-trading features 3Commas lacks, and its beginner UX is arguably cleaner. But 3Commas has better TradingView integration and slightly deeper backtesting. If you want to copy other traders' bots, Cryptohopper wins; if you're building your own strategies, 3Commas wins.

**Pionex** is the free alternative — its bots are built into the exchange itself, so there's no separate subscription. That's great if you're a beginner testing grid bots with small capital, but you're locked into Pionex's own exchange (worse liquidity than Binance) and you can't run the strategy on your existing accounts. 3Commas is the right choice once you're serious enough to want exchange choice.

## The Bottom Line

3Commas is a **legitimate, mature crypto bot platform** that earns its position in the top tier of the category. The Starter tier at $29/month is a fair price for what you get, exchange coverage is genuinely best-in-class, and the TradingView Signal Bot workflow is unmatched for Pine Script users.

The 2022 security incident deserves acknowledgment but shouldn't be disqualifying — the fix is on your side (API key hygiene, no withdrawal permissions, IP whitelisting on the exchange), and every major bot platform faces similar risks by definition. The bigger practical issue is the learning curve: this is not a plug-and-play platform, and beginners without a defined strategy will churn within a month.

If you're an active crypto trader running DCA, grid, or signal-based strategies across multiple exchanges, 3Commas remains one of the two or three platforms worth seriously evaluating in 2026. Start on the free tier, pay for Starter once you've validated one strategy, and only jump to Pro if you're actually trading futures.
pros: 20+ exchange integrations including Binance, Bybit, OKX, Kraken, Coinbase
TradingView Signal Bot integration via Pine Script webhooks
Backtesting runs on full 1-minute historical data
Usable free tier with one DCA bot to test the platform
Annual billing offers roughly 50% discount vs monthly
AI Assistant genuinely helps configure bots for beginners
cons: 2022 phishing-based security incident affected API keys
Crypto only — no stocks, forex, or traditional futures
Steep learning curve, UI has legacy complexity from 2017
Pro tier at $99/month is overpriced unless trading futures actively
No native custom indicators — must route via TradingView
best_for: Active crypto traders using multiple exchanges
TradingView Pine Script users automating signals
DCA and grid bot strategy builders
Portfolio managers running multiple concurrent strategies
